points.text: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS‑CoV‑2.[doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000][doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00024]
The WHO declared COVID-19 a global health emergency in March 2020 and ended the emergency in May 2023.[doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]
COVID-19 is an illness caused by the virus SARS‑CoV‑2 [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]
The virus began spreading at the end of 2019 and was declared a pandemic in 2020 [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]
COVID‑19 spreads mainly through close contact via respiratory droplets when an infected person coughs, sneezes, talks, or breathes [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007
Many infected people have mild or no symptoms, but older adults and those with certain medical conditions can develop severe illness [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_000
COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by the SARS‑CoV‑2 coronavirus; the name stands for corona, virus, disease [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]
